english: because he is descended from . . .; cì byuṅ-ba yin (ci byung ba yin), Glr. what has become of him? de-dus cì byas-pa yin (de dus ci byas pa yin) Mil. what were you doing just then? so esp. W.: *zer-pa yin (zer pa yin), zér-pen* (zer pen) he has said it, *kál-pen* (kal pen) it has been sent off; joined to the partic. fut., (or to the partic. pres. or pf., in as far as these are sometimes used also for the fut.) it expresses futurity: śi-ba yin (shi ba yin) Pth. I shall die; ṅo su śes-pa-la bskur-ba yin (ngo su shes pa la bskur ba yin) Glr. she shall be given to him, that will know her, find her out from amongst the rest; gró-ba yin mod (gro ba yin mod) Glr. indeed you will have to go now. When joined to a root, it is only in W. that it denotes the future: *léb yin (leb yin), léb-bin* (leb bin) he will come, *táṅ yin* (tang yin) he will give. Comp. *yin-tog-can* (yin tog can) W. thinking one's self to be something (great), proud, conceited. - yin-tsul (yin tshul) Mil. property, attribute, ni (ni) f. - yin-lugs (yin lugs) 1. circumstances, condition (= gnas-lugs? (gnas lugs?)); Kóṅ-rnams-kyi yin-lugs brjod (khong rnams kyi yin lugs brjod) Mil. she related to him her circumstances. 2. nature or essence of things Mil.
v. car-yib (char yib) eaves, shed Mil. nt., yet cf. the following.

to hide one's self C., W.; car yib byed-pa (char yib byed pa) Pth. to take shelter from the rain; *yib-te bor-ce* (yib te bor ce) W. to hide, conceal; yib-ma (yib ma) something hidden Sch.; yib-sa (yib sa) place of concealment, hiding-corner.
termination of the instrum. case after vowels, po.
1. sbst.? yu byed-pa (yu byed pa) to calumniate Sch. (?); yu-na (yu na) if it is true Sch. (??) - 2. num. figure: 84.
oats, or a similar kind of grain, which, in case of need, may serve for food C.
officinal tree, yielding a remedy for wounds and sores S.g.; also fig. Wdṅ.

n. of a country and people, Cs., which Sch. gratuitously identifies with Taṅ-gud (tang gud); however Glr. p. 32 is stated, that Tibet derived mathematical science and works of art from the east, viz. China and Minyag (i.e. Taṅ-yud (tang yud)), laws and specimens of workmanship from the north, viz. Hor and Yugera (which are frequently mentioned together Ma.) - a passage which Sch. (History of the Eastern Monguls, 328) translated, but owing to an obscurity in the Mongul text, he failed to recognize Yugera, instead of which he has the word 'Gugi', questionable even to himself. (Sch. on the 'Phantom of the Turkish Uigures', v. Preface to Dzl. IX.).

handle, hilt, shaft, gri-yu (gri yu) haft of a knife; stár-yu (star yu) helve of an axe; débs-yu (debs yu) handle of an awl; lhám-yu (lham yu) leg of a boot Cs.; yú(-ba)-can (yu(-ba)-can) provided with a handle, yu-med (yu med) without a handle Cs.; yu-bcad (yu bcad) 'shoes, slippers' Sch. (?).

ox, cow, having no horns Cs.; for yú-mo (yu mo) Sch. has 'hind, female of a stag'; it seems to be little known. yú-mo srol-goṅ (yu mo srol gong) and yú-mo mdeu-byiṅ (yu mo mde'u bying) names of plants Wdṅ.

(= bubs (bubs)) 1. piece of cloth or stuff; gós-su ras-yúg gcig-las mi bdóg-ste (gos su ras yug gcig las mi bdog ste) Dzl. as they had but one cotton cloth for their clothing. Cotton cloth is generally of very small width, but the silk fabric, designated by dar-yúg (dar yug), seems not to exceed much the breadth of ribbons Glr. - 2. for yud (yud) Mil.
Ld. yúg-pa (yug pa) oats, prob. the same as yú-ku (yu ku).

mourning for a deceased husband or wife, and the state of uncleanness consequent to it, the duration of which varies according to circumstances, whether the first or second spouse has died, and also with respect to the different countries; yúg(s)-sa-pa (yug(s) sa pa), also yúg(s)-sa (yug(s) sa) widower, yúg(s)-sa-mo (yug(s) sa mo) widow; yúg(s)-sa póg-pa (yug(s) sa pog pa) being unclean in consequence of mourning; sáṅs-pa (sangs pa) cleansed, viz. by the expiration of the time of mourning Cs.
